column4: MVP PILOT LOCATION 01: SEHORE DISTRICT (MADHYA PRADESH)

column6:
Action: Perform deep summer plowing to kill soil-borne pests. 

Preparation: Check soil moisture before planning sowing.
column7: Action: Proceed with land conditioning and harrowing to prepare a fine seedbed.
column8: Action: Ideal for final field prep. Ensure drainage channels are clear of debris.
column9: Action: Delay land/soil preparation to avoid it becoming too sticky. Clear stagnant water.
column13: Deep summer plowing to sanitize soil and kill pests.
column14: Prepare for sowing; track Mrug Nakshatra showers for soil conditioning
column15: Delay harrowing, soil will be sticky to work
column16: Stop/Delay soil preparation, high risk of soil structure damage

column6: Action: Delay Sowing (seed will bake in hot/dry soil) as it can be detrimental to the seed. Wait for 75-100mm rain.
column7:
Action: Sow only if soil moisture reaches 3-4 inches deep. 

Preparation: Ensure seed treatment is done.
column8: Action: Perfect onset. Sow now for uniform germination across the whole block. (Check for mud trap risk).
column9: Action: Stop Sowing due to high risk of poor aeration. Heavy rain will cause "Mud Trap." The seed will rot before it can breathe.
column13: Delay sowing as seedling might burn; until 75-100mm cumulative rain is reached.
column14: Risk of poor stands; check "Monsoon Continuity and if 75-100mm threshold met"
column15: Ideal for establishment if reaching 75-100mm threshold.
column16: Stop sowing: High risk of soil compaction

column6: Observe: Heat may bake top layer. High risk of seedling mortality if moisture drops.
column7: Observe: Light rain cools the soil. Monitor for steady sprout emergence.
column8: Observe: Monitor emergence. Ideal moisture for uniform stand.
column9: Alert: Severe Crusting (Papdi). Seedlings trapped under soil, black soil might lock, prepare to break if required.
column13: Action: Monitor for "Seedling Burn" if soil heat is high.
column14: Action: Monitor for "Seedling Burn" if soil heat is high.
column15: Action: Risk: Soil crusting. Monitor for emergence.
column16: Action: Severe Risk: Seedling Rot or "Mud Trap" asphyxiation.

column6: Warning: Soil is drying. If sprouts don't appear by Day 7, prepare for a light sprinkler spray.
column7: Observe: Watch the soil surface to check whether heat baked the top layer or soil, and if the light rain can cool the soil  sprouts.
column8:
Preparation: Weeds (like Sanwa) will emerge soon. Get your "Dora" (inter-culture tool) or herbicides ready.

Check for risk of crusting.
column9: Alert: Severe Crusting (Papdi). Seedlings trapped under soil, black soil might lock, prepare to break if required.

column6: Warning: "Hardening of crop" phase. Let the roots go deep into the Black soil. Do not irrigate yet.; string to be returned if in window t+1 to t-10; string to be returned if in window t+10 to t+ 20
column7: Preparation: Plan for hoeing to conserve the limited moisture available in soil.
column8: Prepare: Growth is steady. Complete weeding before crop branches out.
column9: Alert: High root rot risk. Standing water for 48 hours is fatal. Check drains.

column6: Action: "Hardening of crop" phase. Hardening phase. Use  intercultural tools "Dora" to break capillaries and save deep moisture.; string to be returned if in window t+1 to t-10; string to be returned if in window t+10 to t+ 20
column7: Status: Growth is steady/ideal. Use "Dora" to save moisture and manage early weeds.
column8: Observe: Ideal for steady growth. Check and manage for weed/pest issues
column9: Urgent Action: Waterlogging risk is high. Soybean cannot stand in water for 48 hours. Drain immediately.
column13: Pest Monitoring for thrips/aphids; growth slows.
column15: Manage drainage to prevent Seedling Rot
column16: Risk due to stagnant water causing yellowing/root rot

column6: Action: "Hardening of crop" phase. Hardening phase. Use  intercultural tools "Dora" to break capillaries and save deep moisture.; string to be returned if in window t+1 to t-10; string to be returned if in window t+10 to t+ 20
column7: Status: Growth is steady/ideal. Use "Dora" to save moisture and manage early weeds.
column8: Observe: Ideal for steady growth. Check and manage for weed/pest issues
column9: Urgent Action: Waterlogging risk is high. Soybean cannot stand in water for 48 hours. Drain immediately.

column6: Warning: Drought stress risk next week might cause flower drop. Prepare pumps for 1st Irrigation.
column8: Prepare: Humidity rising. Buy Girdle Beetle sprays before canopy closes.
column9: Alert: Flowering starts next week. Perfect drainage is required to save yield.

column6: Action: Only if required, apply Urea post-rain. Moisture needed to absorb branching nutrients.
column7: Action: Only if required, apply Urea top-dressing while soil is damp for better uptake.
column8: Action: Only if required, apply Urea after rainfall. Ideal window for maximum nutrient uptake.
column9: Warning: High Leaching Risk. Postpone chemicals. Manage runoff to save nutrients.

column6: Action: Perform 1st Life-Saving Irrigation. High shedding risk. Apply water to save pods.
column7: Action: Beneficial moisture. Monitor for early pests. Ideal for pod growth.
column8: Action: Ideal for flowering. Conduct intensive pest surveillance (Aphids). Potential for minor drop. Virus control check.
column9: Warning/Action: Severe Flower Drop. Stagnant water is toxic. Drain field within 24 hrs.
column13: Critical: Use stored water for "life-saving" irrigation to prevent Square Drop.
column16: Prevent Square Drop risk causing physical damage to buds

column6: Action: Perform 2nd Life-Saving Irrigation. Plants need water to push nutrients to pods.
column7: Observe: Ideal condition for growth. Watch for Girdle Beetle activity.
column8: Observe: Watch for Girdle Beetle. Potential pest outbreak due to high humidity.
column9: Warning: Potential pest outbreak due to high humidity risk. Pod drop risk. Keep water exit points clear.

column6: Action: If dry, pods will be flat. Apply moisture to ensure filling.
column7: Action: Ideal filling. Use last moisture to ensure bold grains.
column8: Action: Ideal filling. Monitor for late-stage pests.
column9: Warning: Drainage! Standing water causes "Grain Darkening."
column13: Risk: Reduced boll size. Use irrigation if available.
column16: Shedding risk: Physical flower loss. Drain field.

column6: Action: Perform 3rd Life-Saving Irrigation. Ensure maximum grain weight and market price.
column7: Observe: Ideal for bold grain. Ensure no pests are eating pods.
column8: Observe: Ideal for bold grain. Monitor for diseases due to moisture. Potential Risk of nutrient washout.
column9: Warning: Nutrient leaching is high. Excess water lowers oil content. High runoff risk, drain fast!

column6: Action: Final moisture check. If soil is cracking, provide one last light irrigation.
column7: Observe: Beneficial moisture. Monitor for pests.
column8: Observe: Ideal completion. Monitor for pod-shattering.
column9: Warning: High Risk. Standing water causes pods to rot and seeds to sprout.

column6: Action: Ideal for Drying. Harvest immediately. Dry in sun for 2-3 days.
column7: Action: Delay harvest if pods are damp. Wait for 1 sunny day.
column8: Warning: High pod shattering risk. Harvest with extreme care.
column9: Warning: Risk ofTotal Loss and Sprouting Fungal Rot. Cover heaps immediately.

column15: Risk of Internal Boll Rot. Monitor pink bollworm
column16: Risk: Heavy storms cause "Boll Rot." and fungal infections

column6: Action: Harvesting should be 50% done. Move grain to dry storage.
column7: Action: Protect harvested stacks. Keep grain under cover for color.
column8: Warning: Wet soil staining risk. Wait for field to dry before cutting.
column9: Warning: Stop All Harvest. Standing water destroys quality.

column16: Total Loss. Fiber degrades; pods rot.
